name: Svchost DLL Search Order Hijack Detected
description: Detection of a DLL search order hijack involving `svchost.exe`. Adversaries may place a malicious DLL in a location that is searched before the legitimate one, causing `svchost.exe` to load the malicious DLL instead. This is a common persistence and privilege escalation technique.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: a DLL search order hijack was detected. The process **{source_image}** loaded a suspicious DLL (**{image}**), which is typically associated with a known hijacking technique. This is a strong indicator of **Persistence** and **Privilege Escalation** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1574.001, T1574.002**). Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action and to remove the malicious DLL. The event occurred under the user **{user}**.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: source_image
parameters.description: The process image that loaded the DLL, which is expected to be 'svchost.exe'.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The name of the suspicious DLL that was loaded.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the action.
